root.question: Assess the organizational context based on total developer headcount and compliance/regulatory requirements.

recommend_flat_generalist.reasoning: With under 10 developers, prioritizing communication speed over structural boundaries is critical. Adding 'Squads' or a 'Platform Team' at this stage creates artificial silos, unnecessary overhead, and slows down product-market fit iteration. Keep it flat.

recommend_stream_aligned_transition.reasoning: Between 10 and 40 developers, communication breaks down if kept flat. Transition to Stream-Aligned teams focused on business value. Crucially, begin forming an implicit/early Platform Team (1-2 devs) to standardize CI/CD and prevent tech stack fragmentation.

recommend_mature_platform_stream.strategy: Mature Platform as a Product + Autonomous Streams (>40 Devs)
recommend_mature_platform_stream.reasoning: At 40+ developers, cognitive load and architectural drift become massive risks. You must establish a dedicated Platform Team treating internal developers as customers (Platform-as-a-Product) offering self-service Golden Paths. Stream-aligned teams consume the platform to maintain high Deployment Frequency.

recommend_regulated_platform.strategy: Compliance-Enforced Platform + Stream Teams (>40 Devs, High Regulation)
recommend_regulated_platform.reasoning: Scaling in a highly regulated environment (Fintech, Healthcare) requires the Platform Team to embed Compliance-as-Code (automated SAST/DAST, audit logging, access controls) into the Golden Paths. Teams maintain autonomy only within the boundaries of the pre-approved platform abstractions.
